objective: Define the smallest deterministic harness amendment that makes identical dependency installations root-independent while still rejecting version, manifest-byte, tool, browser, lockfile, config, writer, row, runtime, platform, and CPU drift.
questions: Which absolute fields cause false environment drift across exact roots?
What is the minimum root-independent identity that retains dependency integrity?
What deterministic valid and invalid fixtures prove the correction?
How must the exact same amended harness be used in both roots without changing baseline product source?
anti_goals: Do not edit product, benchmark, tests, docs, Git, PR, or prior evidence.
Do not run captures or comparisons.
Do not weaken manifest bytes, versions, browser binary bytes, lockfile, config, writer, row, runtime, platform, or CPU checks.
Do not propose symlink, NODE_PATH, source-tree import, or result-rewriting workarounds.
Do not claim benchmark or release readiness.
required_sentence: DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
